invariant_definition: Possessing qualities. *Saguṇa brahman* is *brahman* described under qualifying attributes — preeminently as the personal Lord (*Īśvara* / Bhagavān). The *saguṇa*-*nirguṇa* opposition is a fundamental Advaita technical apparatus; non-Advaita schools either reject the opposition (Viśiṣṭādvaita: *brahman* is always *saguṇa*; *nirguṇa* texts deny *heya-guṇas*) or modify it (Dvaita: Viṣṇu has infinite real *kalyāṇa-guṇas*; *nirguṇa* texts deny *prākṛta-guṇas* only).

per_school.definition: *Saguṇa brahman* is *brahman* with *māyā*-upādhi — the cosmic Lord who creates, sustains, dispenses *karma*-fruits. Real for *vyāvahārika* and devotional standpoints; sublatable in *nirguṇa brahman*-realization. *Saguṇa* is therefore the lower (apara) but real description.
*Brahman* is always *saguṇa* — there is no higher *nirguṇa* description. *Nirguṇa* texts deny only *heya-guṇas* (defects).
Viṣṇu possesses infinite real *kalyāṇa-guṇas* (auspicious qualities); *nirguṇa* texts deny only *prākṛta-guṇas* (the qualities of inert matter).

translator_note: 'Qualified' or 'with-qualities' acceptable. Note: *saguṇa* is *not* simply 'personal' in the Christian-theological sense — *saguṇa brahman* in Advaita is a metaphysical category, not a relational one.
